title: subagent-toolkit project feature list
description: The features.json project-state file (harness/context-management.md §6): the machine-readable definition of done for a project. The orchestrator creates it; workers may only flip 'passes' and fill 'evidence' — never edit 'id', 'title', or 'acceptance'. Validate and gate with harness/scripts/check_features.py, which also enforces what a schema can't: evidence paths exist for passing features, and (with --against) that workers didn't touch the criteria.

id.description: Stable slug, unique within the file. Referenced by briefs, reports, and checkpoints.

title.description: One-line human name for the feature.

acceptance.description: The machine-checkable acceptance criterion from the spec — a command, an observable behavior, or a concrete state a test can assert. Owned by the orchestrator; workers never edit it.

passes.description: Whether the acceptance criterion has been verified. Only flipped to true with evidence attached.

evidence.description: Proof-artifact path(s) — test output, E2E log, screenshot — backing passes: true. A string path, an array of them, or null while passes is false. Required in practice when passes is true; check_features.py enforces existence and non-emptiness.

milestone.description: Optional milestone tag so gates can check one milestone at a time (check_features.py --gate --milestone M2).
